reasoning_model_1: Model 1 demonstrates perfect content accuracy, reading the script exactly as provided without any additions or omissions. Regarding the required paralinguistic feature 'Pause' (停顿), the model correctly identifies the location after the word '成功' (Success) within the sentence ('...终于研发成功 [Pause] 并且...'). Based on the generated text flow, there is a clear syntactic break and pause introduced at the 00:06 mark between '成功' and '并且', which aligns well with the instruction to pause obviously. The overall prosody is natural and fluent.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails in content accuracy. It hallucinates and reads the instruction keyword '成功' at the very beginning of the audio (00:00) before starting the actual script at 00:01. This indicates a failure to distinguish the prompt instruction from the text to be synthesized (prompt leakage). While the model does read the full script afterwards and likely includes a pause after the internal word '成功' (around 00:07-00:08), the initial content error is a significant flaw that violates the accuracy criterion.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately synthesizes the text and adheres to the paralinguistic instruction by placing a pause at the correct location within the sentence. Model 2 commits a critical error by reading part of the instruction ('成功') aloud at the start of the audio, resulting in a content discrepancy. Although Model 2 eventually reads the script, the inclusion of extra words lowers its score significantly compared to the flawless execution of Model 1.
